conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first one:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I'm asking about my son Alex. He's 19 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex. Since Alex is 19 years old, I need to understand a bit more about his situation, as Child Benefit eligibility for young people aged 16-20 depends on their circumstances.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he in full-time non-advanced education, such as studying for A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar qualifications?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is studying for his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is studying for his A-levels, I need to understand a bit more about the nature of his studies.

**Is Alex studying full-time (at least 12 hours a week of supervised study)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's studying full-time. It's a proper full-time A-level course.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to understand your relationship to Alex and your living situation.

**Are you Alex's parent or the person responsible for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm his parent. He lives here at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I need to ask about your circumstances:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have a few more questions to ensure I have a complete picture.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! Based on the information you've provided, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 19 years old and studying full-time A-levels (which counts as approved full-time non-advanced education)
- Child Benefit can be claimed for young people aged 16-20 if they're in approved education or training
- You are Alex's parent and he lives with you in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the key eligibility requirements. You can apply for Child Benefit for Alex, and the benefit will continue until he turns 20 or leaves approved education or training, whichever comes first.

Please note that while you're eligible to receive Child Benefit, you should also be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £50,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge through your tax return. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 19 years old and studying full-time A-levels (at least 12 hours a week of supervised study). According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can be claimed for young people aged 16-20 if they are in approved education or training. A-levels count as approved full-time non-advanced education. The claimant is Alex's parent, they live together in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the key eligibility criteria are met: (1) the child is under 20 and in approved education, (2) the claimant is responsible for the child, (3) they live in the UK, and (4) no one else is claiming for this child.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is Alex's parent, living in the UK with their 19-year-old son who is studying full-time A-levels. Since Alex is under 20 and in approved full-time non-advanced education, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, the claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
